false_negatives_scope_b.name: faker
false_negatives_scope_b.notes: EXPECTED FN: Community fork has good governance now - original incident not detectable.

false_negatives_scope_b.name: node-ipc
false_negatives_scope_b.notes: EXPECTED FN: Active maintainer sabotage - governance metrics won't catch active projects.

false_negatives_scope_b.name: devise
false_negatives_scope_b.notes: Governance risk: Critical Rails auth library, original creator (José Valim) moved on. Single active maintainer (Carlos da Silva) at 81% concentration, 42 commits/yr.

false_negatives_scope_b.name: polyfillpolyfill/polyfill-library
false_negatives_scope_b.notes: Domain/project sold to Funnull CDN who injected malicious JS into 100K+ sites. Governance failure: ownership transfer without safeguards. Scores 40 — partial detection of underlying risk but ownership transfer itself is not a tracked signal.

false_negatives_scope_b.name: core-js
false_negatives_scope_b.notes: Governance risk: sole maintainer (zloirock), 92% concentration. Was imprisoned Jan-Oct 2020 (7-month release gap). No malicious release occurred. Currently active (1174 commits/yr) so moderate score is defensible, but bus factor 1 with 26M weekly downloads remains a risk.

false_negatives_scope_b.name: es5-ext
false_negatives_scope_b.notes: Protestware: maintainer (medikoo) added postinstall script showing anti-war message to Russian-locale users. Non-destructive but unilateral. Bus factor 1, 13M weekly downloads.

false_negatives_scope_b.name: is-promise
false_negatives_scope_b.notes: Non-malicious: sole maintainer published broken ESM migration (missing ./ in exports). Broke create-react-app, firebase-tools, Angular CLI. Fixed in 3 hours. Bus factor 1, 766 direct dependents, 3.4M GitHub repos. Demonstrates governance fragility.

false_negatives_scope_b.name: telnyx
false_negatives_scope_b.notes: NEAR-MISS FN at 55 (threshold 60). TeamPCP campaign — maintainer-account compromise, payload steganographically hidden in WAV file (matched the SDK's voice-AI use case). Versions 4.87.1/4.87.2. Governance weakness present (bus factor 1, 97% concentration, 78% attrition) but org backing (-15) softens score below threshold. Illustrates the boundary: vendor-backed solo projects sit at the edge of what concentration+activity scoring can flag.

false_positives.name: rxjs
false_positives.notes: Google Angular team involvement, professional development

case.notes: Infrastructure as Code. HashiCorp, corporate backing, professional governance.

case.name: strong_password
case.notes: RubyGems account compromise. Single maintainer, small package.

case.name: cline
case.notes: EXPECTED FN: Account compromise via clinebotorg npm account. Malicious postinstall silently installed openclaw backdoor. Well-governed project (256 contributors, 58K stars). Detected by missing provenance attestations — a signal ossuary doesn't track.

case.name: polyfillpolyfill/polyfill-library
case.notes: Domain/project sold to Funnull CDN who injected malicious JS into 100K+ sites. Governance failure: ownership transfer without safeguards. Scores 40 — partial detection of underlying risk but ownership transfer itself is not a tracked signal.

case.name: reviewdog/action-setup
case.notes: EXPECTED FN: CI/CD access policy exploit — contributors had automatic write access. Malicious actor modified v1 tag to dump secrets. Led to tj-actions cascade. Well-maintained org project; the issue was CI permissions, not governance.

case.name: solana-labs/solana-web3.js
case.notes: EXPECTED FN: Maintainer spear-phished via fake npm site. Malicious versions exfiltrated Solana private keys. $130-160K stolen in ~5 hours. Well-governed project — pure credential theft.

case.name: ultralytics
case.notes: EXPECTED FN: GitHub Actions workflow vulnerability allowed malicious PR branch name to execute shell commands. Hit twice in 36 hours because tokens weren't rotated. Well-governed org project.

case.name: codecov/codecov-action
case.notes: EXPECTED FN: Build infra compromise — HMAC keys extracted from Docker image to modify bash uploader in GCS. Exfiltrated CI env vars for 2+ months undetected. Well-governed project with corporate backing (Codecov had 23K customers).

case.name: web-infra-dev/rspack
case.notes: EXPECTED FN: CI/CD misconfiguration — 'Release Canary' workflow ran attacker code on comment keywords. npm token stolen, XMRig published in @rspack/core v1.1.7. Active project with ByteDance backing.

case.name: chalk
case.notes: EXPECTED FN: Qix account phished via fake npmjs.help domain. Malicious versions of chalk + 16 packages (2B weekly downloads) intercepted crypto wallet activity. Mitigated within ~2 hours. Sindre Sorhus project with strong governance.

case.name: figlief/ctx
case.notes: Attacker purchased expired domain figlief.com, used PyPI password reset to claim maintainer account. Published malicious versions stealing env vars. Single maintainer, years of inactivity — classic abandonment signal.

case.name: boltdb/bolt
case.notes: Archived since 2018, single maintainer. Succeeded by etcd-io/bbolt. The abandonment enabled a typosquat (boltdb-go/bolt) that persisted 3+ years in Go Module Mirror cache. Classic governance risk: abandoned but still depended on.

case.name: is
case.notes: Old inactive maintainer's account phished, still had publish access. Malicious versions undetected for 6 hours. Single maintainer, very low activity — governance weakness correctly detected despite credential theft being the attack vector.

case.name: num2words
case.notes: EXPECTED FN: Org-backed (savoirfairelinux), 15% concentration, 20 commits/yr. Governance was healthy — pure credential phishing, outside detection scope.

case.name: tj-actions/changed-files
case.notes: EXPECTED FN: Multi-stage CI/CD cascade: SpotBugs pull_request_target vuln → stolen PAT → reviewdog compromise → tj-actions compromise. Dumped CI runner memory across 23K+ repos. Well-maintained project; attack exploited CI trust chains.

case.name: eslint-config-prettier
case.notes: EXPECTED FN: Maintainer JounQin phished via npnjs.com (typosquatted npm domain). Malicious versions of eslint-config-prettier + 5 related packages (78M weekly downloads). Well-maintained Prettier org project — pure credential theft.

case.name: nrwl/nx
case.notes: EXPECTED FN: Exploited pull_request_target workflow on old branch. 8 malicious versions stole SSH keys, tokens, crypto wallets. Also attempted to co-opt local AI CLI tools for recon. Very healthy governance (many contributors, active org).

case.name: core-js
case.notes: Governance risk: sole maintainer (zloirock), 92% concentration. Was imprisoned Jan-Oct 2020 (7-month release gap). No malicious release occurred. Currently active (1174 commits/yr) so moderate score is defensible, but bus factor 1 with 26M weekly downloads remains a risk.

case.name: es5-ext
case.notes: Protestware: maintainer (medikoo) added postinstall script showing anti-war message to Russian-locale users. Non-destructive but unilateral. Bus factor 1, 13M weekly downloads.

case.name: event-source-polyfill
case.notes: Protestware: maintainer (Yaffle) added runtime anti-war message for Russian users. Non-destructive. Bus factor 1, 600K weekly downloads, 135K GitHub repos.

case.name: purescript
case.notes: Maintainer shinnn sabotaged dependency packages (load-from-cwd-or-npm, rate-map) after governance dispute with PureScript core team. Bus factor 1 on those deps. Discovered July 9, resolved mid-July 2019.

case.name: is-promise
case.notes: Non-malicious: sole maintainer published broken ESM migration (missing ./ in exports). Broke create-react-app, firebase-tools, Angular CLI. Fixed in 3 hours. Bus factor 1, 766 direct dependents, 3.4M GitHub repos. Demonstrates governance fragility.

case.name: jteeuwen/go-bindata
case.notes: Username recycling: original author deleted GitHub account, attacker re-registered 'jteeuwen' and pushed malicious code. Classic abandoned project takeover via namespace hijacking.

case.name: axios
case.notes: EXPECTED FN: Account compromise via long-lived npm token. Malicious versions 1.14.1/0.30.4 injected plain-crypto-js RAT. Healthy governance (19% conc, 223 commits/yr, org-owned, bf=6). Detected by Socket in 6 minutes.

case.name: aquasecurity/trivy-action
case.notes: EXPECTED FN: CI/CD compromise via stolen credential with write access. Attacker force-pushed 75/76 version tags to inject TeamPCP stealer. Well-governed project (Aqua Security org). Attack vector is credential theft + CI/CD tag manipulation, not governance weakness.

case.name: aquasecurity/trivy
case.notes: Container security scanner. Aqua Security org, 3 core contributors, 573 commits/yr. Bus factor 3, elephant factor 1 (org-dominated). Well-governed despite org concentration.

case.name: litellm
case.notes: EXPECTED FN: TeamPCP campaign — PYPI_PUBLISH token exfiltrated via compromised Trivy CI step. Malicious versions 1.82.7/1.82.8 deployed litellm_init.pth that runs on every Python startup, K8s privileged pods, systemd backdoor. Healthy Ossuary governance (21% conc, ~16K commits/yr, 1016 contributors, bf=4, BerriAI org). Underlying weakness (unpinned CI deps, broad publish tokens) is a CI hygiene issue Ossuary does not measure.

case.name: telnyx
case.notes: NEAR-MISS FN at 55 (threshold 60). TeamPCP campaign — maintainer-account compromise, payload steganographically hidden in WAV file (matched the SDK's voice-AI use case). Versions 4.87.1/4.87.2. Governance weakness present (bus factor 1, 97% concentration, 78% attrition) but org backing (-15) softens score below threshold. Illustrates the boundary: vendor-backed solo projects sit at the edge of what concentration+activity scoring can flag.

case.name: xinference
case.notes: EXPECTED FN: TeamPCP-style supply-chain attack. Malicious versions 2.6.0–2.6.2 shipped Base64 second-stage collector harvesting browser-extension wallets (MetaMask, Phantom), local crypto wallets, npm tokens. Marker '# hacked by teampcp' in decoded payload (TeamPCP claimed copycat). Healthy governance (19% conc, 596 commits/yr, 51 contributors, bf=4, Xprobe org). Score 0 (VERY_LOW). Same T4 profile as axios.
